description: Scheduled tasks managed by Cowork's native Scheduled Tasks panel. These tasks run independent of any active session. Set each task up in Cowork via /schedule — use the cowork_prompt field as the task prompt. Mark configured: true after setup.
setup: Run /rigby scheduled-setup for copy-paste setup cards.

tasks.name: Morning Briefing
tasks.description: Weekday morning briefing — prose narrative synthesis of the day's calendar, priorities, overdue delegations, and rock alignment. Delivered at 6:30am before the day starts.
tasks.cowork_prompt: Run the chief-morning skill to deliver the morning briefing. Pull live calendar, tasks, delegations, and meeting context for today. Synthesize everything into a prose narrative briefing — three paragraphs covering the day's character, execution reality, and sharp edge — followed by the calendar table. End with 'What do you want to tackle first?'

tasks.name: Auto Daily Review
tasks.description: Autonomous nightly review — synthesizes yesterday's task completions, calendar, and system state into a first-person narrative journal entry written to the knowledge system only. Runs at 4am without user input.
tasks.cowork_prompt: Run the chief-review skill with arguments: auto. Pull task completions, calendar events, inbox state, delegation status, and quarterly rock context from yesterday. Synthesize into a first-person prose narrative journal entry and write it to the knowledge system. No user interaction. Deliver a two-line confirmation when done.
tasks.setup_note: Requires Keep Awake to be enabled in Cowork — this task fires at 4am while you're asleep.

tasks.name: Weekly Review Prep
tasks.description: Sunday morning weekly review prep — rocks, OmniFocus health, delegations, relationship warmth, system error analysis, commitment gap detection across all sources, and pattern analysis. Delivered at 8am before the week begins.
tasks.cowork_prompt: Run the quinn-weekly-review skill to prepare the weekly review document. Pull quarterly rock status, OmniFocus health, delegation review, calendar analysis, email signals, relationship warmth via Clay, system error analysis, and run the commitment gap detection step across all available sources (Teams transcripts, Teams chat, email, Plaud transcripts in Obsidian, OmniFocus). Scan previous weekly review files for recurring gap patterns. Write the full output to reviews/weekly-review-prep-{date}.md.

tasks.name: Wine Monitor — Hour of Power
tasks.description: Last Bottle Wine Monitor — starts daemon at the Hour of Power (highest priority window for cult Napa Cab, collectibles, library vintages). Polls every 2-3 minutes through remaining drop windows and alerts via Slack DM when a match is found.
tasks.cowork_prompt: Run: python3 /home/user/Jarvis/systems/wine-monitor/monitor.py --daemon. This starts the Last Bottle wine monitor in daemon mode. It will poll lastbottlewines.com every 2-10 minutes depending on the active drop window and send Slack DM alerts when wines matching David's taste profile appear. Let it run — it auto-adjusts polling frequency and stops at midnight.
tasks.setup_note: Daemon runs from 1 PM through midnight. For earlier coverage (11 AM Pinot Hour), add a second scheduled task at 10:55 AM or install system cron via: bash /home/user/Jarvis/systems/wine-monitor/install.sh

tasks.name: Jarvis Channel Poller
tasks.description: Polls #jarvis Slack channel for messages from David. Routes them to the Jarvis master agent via Anthropic API and posts responses back as Jarvis bot. Adaptive polling: 5min idle, ramps to 30s during active conversation, backs off after silence, returns to 5min default after 5 minutes with no David message. Auto-compacts conversation context at 80% usage.
tasks.cowork_prompt: Run: python3 /Users/davidohara/develop/jarvis/systems/jarvis-channel/jarvis-channel.py — this is a long-running daemon. It will poll #jarvis for David messages, pass them to Jarvis via the Anthropic API, and post responses back as the Jarvis bot. It manages its own polling interval and will run until stopped.
tasks.setup_note: Requires ANTHROPIC_API_KEY in config/.env. Run install.sh first: bash /Users/davidohara/develop/jarvis/systems/jarvis-channel/install.sh
